（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}})Although generally considered to belong to the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}}, some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#関連項目]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" section of the "{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏}}''' (なすうじ, {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}}, a samurai family from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, famous for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一}}, who shot the fan target in the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い}}. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#関連項目]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}** * A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** * A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt==Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:redirect]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]]

See also [[#関連項目]].）

Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}** * A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** * A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:redirect]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]]

See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" section of the "{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏}}''' (なすうじ, {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}}, a samurai family from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, famous for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一}}, who shot the fan target in the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い}}. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}** * A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** * A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:redirect]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]]

According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** * A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]]

etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" section of the "{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏}}''' (なすうじ, {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}}, a samurai family from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, famous for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一}}, who shot the fan target in the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い}}. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}** * A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** * A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]]

See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" section of the "{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏}}''' (なすうじ, {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}}, a samurai family from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, famous for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一}}, who shot the fan target in the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い}}. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}** * A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** * A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

See also [[#See Also]].）

Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}{{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

{{日本の氏族 |家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (那須国造) |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏 (古代氏族)}} |家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}
（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, the 8th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |出身地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}} |根拠地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏}}（{{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}})Although generally considered to belong to the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}}, some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" section of the "{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏}}''' (なすうじ, {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}}, a samurai family from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, famous for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一}}, who shot the fan target in the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い}}. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}{{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}{{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (那須国造) |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏 (古代氏族)}} |家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}
（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, the 8th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |出身地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}} |根拠地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏}}（{{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家}})Although generally considered to belong to the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}}, some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" section of the "{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇}}, the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏}}''' (なすうじ, {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}}, a samurai family from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, famous for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一}}, who shot the fan target in the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い}}. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}{{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

{{日本の氏族 |家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (那須国造) |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏 (古代氏族)|12=draft}} |家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=draft}}
（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|12=draft}}, the 8th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=draft}} |出身地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}} |根拠地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏|12=draft}}（{{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=draft}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=draft}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=draft}})Although generally considered to belong to the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|12=draft}}, some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=draft}}" section of the "{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=draft}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=draft}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|12=draft}}, the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}}''' (なすうじ, {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=draft}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}}, a samurai family from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, famous for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一|12=draft}}, who shot the fan target in the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=draft}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=draft}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=draft}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=draft}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人|12=draft}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川|12=draft}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙|12=draft}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡|12=draft}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|12=draft}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=draft}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=draft}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=draft}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家|12=draft}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

See also [[#See Also]].）

The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙|12=draft}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡|12=draft}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|12=draft}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=draft}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=draft}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=draft}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家|12=draft}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (那須国造) |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏 (古代氏族)|12=draft}} |家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=draft}}
（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|12=draft}}, the 8th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=draft}} |出身地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}} |根拠地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏|12=draft}}（{{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=draft}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=draft}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=draft}})Although generally considered to belong to the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|12=draft}}, some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=draft}}" section of the "{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=draft}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=draft}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|12=draft}}, the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}}''' (なすうじ, {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=draft}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}}, a samurai family from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, famous for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一|12=draft}}, who shot the fan target in the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=draft}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=draft}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=draft}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=draft}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人|12=draft}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川|12=draft}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙|12=draft}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡|12=draft}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|12=draft}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=draft}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=draft}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=draft}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家|12=draft}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=draft}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=draft}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=draft}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=draft}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人|12=draft}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川|12=draft}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙|12=draft}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡|12=draft}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|12=draft}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=draft}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=draft}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=draft}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家|12=draft}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

{{日本の氏族 |家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (那須国造) |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏 (古代氏族)|12=draft}} |家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=draft}}
（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|12=draft}}, the 8th {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=draft}} |出身地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}} |根拠地 = {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏|12=draft}}（{{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=draft}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=draft}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=draft}})Although generally considered to belong to the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|12=draft}}, some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=draft}}" section of the "{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=draft}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=draft}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko no Mikoto|ja|大彦命|12=draft}}, the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|ja|孝元天皇|12=draft}}, the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}}''' (なすうじ, {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}}: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=draft}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}}, a samurai family from the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, famous for {{ill|Nasu no Yoichi|ja|那須与一|12=draft}}, who shot the fan target in the {{ill|Battle of Yashima|ja|屋島の戦い|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=draft}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=draft}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=draft}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=draft}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=draft}} (see {{ill|Mahito (kabane)|ja|真人|12=draft}}). According to the "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the {{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|ja|那須郡|12=draft}}, particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=draft}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the {{ill|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)|ja|那珂川|12=draft}}, covering most of present-day {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|12=draft}}. The site of the district's {{ill|Gunka (district headquarters)|ja|郡衙|12=draft}} is believed to be the '''{{ill|Nasu Kanga Ruins|ja|那須官衙遺跡|12=draft}}''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町|12=draft}}, adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōtawara, Tochigi|ja|大田原市|12=draft}} enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=draft}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}}. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=draft}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=draft}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=draft}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}}'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=draft}}** ** A lord of the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, ruler of the {{ill|Shirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=draft}} - Though they claim descent from the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家|12=draft}}, some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{citation needed|Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[sn:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}
