However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})Although generally considered to belong to the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the "[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]," during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}''' (なすうじ, [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (ancient)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}, a samurai family from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], famous for [[:en:Nasu no Yoichi|Nasu no Yoichi]], who shot the fan target in the [[:en:Battle of Yashima|Battle of Yashima]]. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokitsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō (kabane)|1=Kō (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131554777}} (see [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito (kabane)]]). According to the "[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the fourth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)]], covering most of present-day [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]]. The site of the district's [[:en:Gunga (Japan)|Gunka (district headquarters)]] is believed to be the '''[[:en:Nasu Kanga ruins|Nasu Kanga Ruins]]''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]] enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], by [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]]. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizamizuka Kofun|1=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990203}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozamizuka Kofun|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the "[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]," during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}''' (なすうじ, [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (ancient)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}, a samurai family from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], famous for [[:en:Nasu no Yoichi|Nasu no Yoichi]], who shot the fan target in the [[:en:Battle of Yashima|Battle of Yashima]]. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokitsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō (kabane)|1=Kō (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131554777}} (see [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito (kabane)]]). According to the "[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the fourth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)]], covering most of present-day [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]]. The site of the district's [[:en:Gunga (Japan)|Gunka (district headquarters)]] is believed to be the '''[[:en:Nasu Kanga ruins|Nasu Kanga Ruins]]''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]] enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], by [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]]. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizamizuka Kofun|1=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990203}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozamizuka Kofun|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:那須国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11642909]] Wikidata name: Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の氏族 |家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (那須国造) |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏 (古代氏族)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |出身地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |根拠地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruta clan|1=Saruta clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572146}}（[[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})Although generally considered to belong to the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the "[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]," during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}''' (なすうじ, [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (ancient)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}, a samurai family from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], famous for [[:en:Nasu no Yoichi|Nasu no Yoichi]], who shot the fan target in the [[:en:Battle of Yashima|Battle of Yashima]]. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokitsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō (kabane)|1=Kō (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131554777}} (see [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito (kabane)]]). According to the "[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the fourth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)]], covering most of present-day [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]]. The site of the district's [[:en:Gunga (Japan)|Gunka (district headquarters)]] is believed to be the '''[[:en:Nasu Kanga ruins|Nasu Kanga Ruins]]''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]] enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], by [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]]. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizamizuka Kofun|1=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990203}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozamizuka Kofun|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

See also [[#See Also]].）

（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |出身地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |根拠地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruta clan|1=Saruta clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572146}}（[[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})Although generally considered to belong to the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the "[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]," during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}''' (なすうじ, [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (ancient)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}, a samurai family from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], famous for [[:en:Nasu no Yoichi|Nasu no Yoichi]], who shot the fan target in the [[:en:Battle of Yashima|Battle of Yashima]]. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokitsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō (kabane)|1=Kō (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131554777}} (see [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito (kabane)]]). According to the "[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the fourth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)]], covering most of present-day [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]]. The site of the district's [[:en:Gunga (Japan)|Gunka (district headquarters)]] is believed to be the '''[[:en:Nasu Kanga ruins|Nasu Kanga Ruins]]''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]] enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], by [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]]. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizamizuka Kofun|1=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990203}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozamizuka Kofun|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the "[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]," during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}''' (なすうじ, [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (ancient)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}, a samurai family from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], famous for [[:en:Nasu no Yoichi|Nasu no Yoichi]], who shot the fan target in the [[:en:Battle of Yashima|Battle of Yashima]]. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokitsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō (kabane)|1=Kō (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131554777}} (see [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito (kabane)]]). According to the "[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the fourth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)]], covering most of present-day [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]]. The site of the district's [[:en:Gunga (Japan)|Gunka (district headquarters)]] is believed to be the '''[[:en:Nasu Kanga ruins|Nasu Kanga Ruins]]''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]] enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], by [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]]. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizamizuka Kofun|1=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990203}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozamizuka Kofun|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:那須国造]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[ja:那須国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11642909]] Wikidata name: Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の氏族 |家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (那須国造) |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏 (古代氏族)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |出身地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |根拠地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruta clan|1=Saruta clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572146}}（[[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})Although generally considered to belong to the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the "[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]," during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}''' (なすうじ, [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (ancient)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}, a samurai family from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], famous for [[:en:Nasu no Yoichi|Nasu no Yoichi]], who shot the fan target in the [[:en:Battle of Yashima|Battle of Yashima]]. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokitsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō (kabane)|1=Kō (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131554777}} (see [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito (kabane)]]). According to the "[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the fourth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)]], covering most of present-day [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]]. The site of the district's [[:en:Gunga (Japan)|Gunka (district headquarters)]] is believed to be the '''[[:en:Nasu Kanga ruins|Nasu Kanga Ruins]]''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]] enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], by [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]]. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizamizuka Kofun|1=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990203}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozamizuka Kofun|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:那須国造]] [[Category:Abe Clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:那須国造]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[ja:那須国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11642909]] Wikidata name: Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の氏族 |家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (那須国造) |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏 (古代氏族)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |出身地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |根拠地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruta clan|1=Saruta clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572146}}（[[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})Although generally considered to belong to the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）
etc. }} '''Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (那須国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] that ruled over what later became the northeastern region of [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the "[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]," during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] (the 12th emperor), '''{{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja|建沼河命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatenuma Kawano Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武渟川別|1=Takenunakawawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546050}} (who was a son of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th emperor), was appointed as the first Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Clan === The '''{{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}''' (なすうじ, [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}) is said to be of the same lineage as the {{ill|Abe clan (ancient)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (ancient)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, although some sources claim they became related later. Another well-known clan of the same name is the {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}}, a samurai family from the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], famous for [[:en:Nasu no Yoichi|Nasu no Yoichi]], who shot the fan target in the [[:en:Battle of Yashima|Battle of Yashima]]. Some theories suggest that this samurai Nasu clan descends from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko line (see [[#See Also]]). Another theory suggests that the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko was related to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.See the page on {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. This is inferred from the inscription on the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, which states, "仰惟殞公廣氏尊胤" ("We revere Lord Un, descendant of the noble Hiroshi"), where "Hiroshi" is interpreted as an abbreviation for {{ill|Hirokitsu clan|ja|広来津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokitsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}},The Hirokitsu clan bore the title {{ill|Kō (kabane)|ja|公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō (kabane)|1=Kō (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131554777}} (see [[:en:Mahito (title)|Mahito (kabane)]]). According to the "[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]," they were descendants of {{ill|Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the fourth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], the 10th emperor. the same lineage as the Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko. == Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuni no Miyatsuko was in what later became the [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], particularly in the area known as '''{{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''."[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Ancient Period, Vol. 2]]". This corresponds to the basin of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Nakagawa River (Tochigi)]], covering most of present-day [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]]. The site of the district's [[:en:Gunga (Japan)|Gunka (district headquarters)]] is believed to be the '''[[:en:Nasu Kanga ruins|Nasu Kanga Ruins]]''', a designated National Historic Site, located in modern [[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa, Tochigi]], adjacent to Ōtawara. == Shrine == The ancestral shrine (氏神) of the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown. However, {{ill|Kasai Shrine|ja|笠石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara, Tochigi]] enshrines the {{ill|Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|ja|那須国造碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument|1=Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Hi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642908}}, a National Treasure, as its shintai (sacred object). The shrine itself was established much later, during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], by [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]]. == Tomb == Tokugawa Mitsukuni identified the '''{{ill|Kamizamizuka Kofun|ja|上侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamizamizuka Kofun|1=Kamisamuraizuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990203}}''' and the '''{{ill|Shimozamizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozamizuka Kofun|1=Shimousamurai Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360620}}''', both keyhole-shaped kofun (前方後方墳), as the possible burial sites of {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a ruler of Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko, and his father Ishi-maro, as mentioned in the monument. However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:那須国造]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})Although generally considered to belong to the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
See also [[#See Also]].）

However, no inscriptions or burial records confirming this link were found during the excavation. The kofun date to the 5th century, much earlier than the 7th-century monument. == Notable Figures == * **{{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A notable regional chieftain of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. According to the Nasu Kuni no Miyatsuko Monument, he was appointed as the **Administrator of Nasu District (評督)** in the first year of {{ill|Eishō (Tang dynasty)|ja|永昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eishō (Tang dynasty)|1=永昌|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11132152}} (689 AD, the 3rd year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]'s reign). == Related Clans == * **{{ill|Nasu Sekiryū|ja|那須赤竜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Sekiryū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}** ** A lord of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], ruler of the [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]], and ancestor of the {{ill|Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja|陸奥氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Ōshū)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造#一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} - Though they claim descent from the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], some theories suggest they descend from the ancient Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite book|last=Ota|first=Ryo|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} {{translated page|ja|那須国造|version=90753199|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:那須国造]] {{wikidata link|Q11642909}} [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:那須国造]] [[ja:那須国造]] [[ja:那須国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11642909]] Wikidata name: Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の氏族 |家名 = Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko (那須国造) |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Nasu clan (ancient)|ja|那須氏 (古代氏族)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (ancient)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |家祖 = {{ill|Ōomi no Mikoto|ja|大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（Descendant of {{ill|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|大彦命|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the first prince of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], the 8th [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]） |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |出身地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |根拠地 = [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District, Tochigi]], {{ill|Nasu Township|ja|那須郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |人物 = {{ill|Nasu Ittei|ja|那須韋提|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Ittei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |支流 = {{ill|Saruta clan|ja|猿田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruta clan|1=Saruta clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572146}}（[[:en:Kabane|Kabane]]: {{ill|Ason|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}）
{{ill|Ashino clan|ja|芦野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashino clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
（{{ill|Nasu clan (samurai)|ja|那須氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu clan (samurai)|1=Nasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642934}} ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})Although generally considered to belong to the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]], some theories claim descent from the Nasu no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
